name: Richard Himber
description: American bandleader, composer, violinist, magician and practical joker (1900–1966)
abstract: Richard Himber (born Herbert Richard Imber; February 20, 1899 – December 11, 1966) was an American bandleader, composer, violinist, magician and practical joker.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: I did a few things, what free lanced Himber... Oh, I worked for Dick Himber, I did a lot of things for Dick Himber on that little group he had called Studebaker Champion.

about_quotes.speaker: Godfrey Hirsch
about_quotes.evidence: himber needed a bi-player. And he heard me and, uh, he came in one night with his, uh, with his attorney... Dick came over to me... can you play with Bull Mallets?

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: I don't think it was Himber, I think he had another guy there rehearsing the band for him... I sat there and made a few mental notes... I learned by listening.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember Richard Himber. [INTERVIEWEE] Richard Himber, there was Jack Sadey...

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: Dick Himber wouldn’t go on the air without me. ... He wouldn’t go anywhere without me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
